recalls.Description: Sanven Technology has announced a recall of VEVOR Garment Steamers, which are designed for wrinkle removal on clothing and textiles. These steamers feature an oval steam vent opening and a white body with 'VEVOR' and 'TOUGH TOOLS, HALF PRICE' printed in orange. A red toggle power switch is located on the handle, and identifying labels on the bottom include 'VEVOR,' 'Garment Steamer,' 'FCL-H09,' and the manufacturer's name, Guangdong ESINO Technology Group Co., Ltd. Each recalled unit was sold with a silver glove accessory.
recalls.Title: Sanven Technology Issues Recall for VEVOR Garment Steamers Due to Burn Hazard Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: Consumers should immediately cease using the recalled steamers and reach out to Sanven Technology at 855-599-6320 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. PT, Monday through Friday, or via email at recalling@vevor.com. Additional information can be found online at vevor.com/pages/vevor-steamer-recall or by visiting www.vevor.com and selecting 'Product Recall' at the top of the page.

Images.Caption: Recalled VEVOR Garment Steamer – side view
Recalled VEVOR Garment Steamer – front view
Recalled VEVOR Garment Steamer – handle view
Label on underside of recalled VEVOR Garment Steamer
Glove sold with recalled VEVOR Garment Steamer

Injuries.Name: The Firm has received six reports of hot water spitting, pouring out, leaking or dripping from the steamers, and 12 reports of issues with the water tank cap detaching, leaking, not staying closed, fitting loosely, or melting. The reported cap issues resulted in two burn injuries, including one consumer who received a permanent scar and a second-degree burn from the hot water, after the steamer's cap suddenly detached and one consumer who received a minor burn on their hand and fingers from leaked water. Additionally, there is one report of a minor steam burn through the provided glove.

Retailers.Name: Available online at Vevor.com, Amazon.com, and HomeDepot.com from December 2023 to September 2025, priced between $10 and $33.

Distributors.Name: Sanven Technology Ltd., dba VEVOR, of Rancho Cucamonga, California

Hazards.Name: Risk of serious burn injury due to leaking or spitting hot water from the steamers.

Remedies.Name: Consumers are advised to stop using the recalled steamers and contact Sanven Technology for a full refund, providing their contact details and a photo of the steamer with its unplugged cut power cord.

recalls.metaDescription: Sanven Technology recalls VEVOR Garment Steamers due to burn hazards. Stop using immediately and contact for refunds.

recalls.Description: Deale International is recalling the CasaClean Handheld Steamers, model DF-958, which come in white/gray and blue/gray color options. These steamers are designed to eliminate dirt, grease, and limescale from various surfaces in homes. Each unit features a water tank, cap, valve assembly, hose, hose clamp, and exit nozzle, with the CasaClean logo displayed in silver on the right side. The model number can be found on a prominent black bar on the product rating sticker located at the bottom of the steamer.
recalls.Title: Deale International Issues Recall for CasaClean Handheld Steamers Due to Burn Injury Risk; Available on HSN
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Deale International toll-free at 877-880-6137 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, email casacleanrecall@thedeale.com, or visit www.thedeale.com/pages/casaclean-safety-recall. Additional information is available at www.thedeale.com by selecting 'RECALL INFORMATION' at the top of the page.

Images.Caption: Recalled Model DF-958 CasaClean Handheld Steamer in White/Gray
Recalled Model DF-958 CasaClean Handheld Steamer in Blue/Gray

Injuries.Name: Deale has received 26 reports of the recalled steamers leaking water or steam, including 13 consumers receiving burns.

Retailers.Name: HSN, sold from November 2024 through April 2025 for approximately $40.

Importers.Name: Deale International LLC, of Fort Lauderdale, Florida

Hazards.Name: Risk of serious burn injury due to steam leakage or hot boiling water discharge.

Remedies.Name: Consumers must cease use of the recalled steamers and contact Deale for guidance on safely cutting the cord near the base. Proof of destruction is required for a refund, which includes uploading a photo of the cut cord along with contact details via the Recall Request form at www.thedeale.com/pages/casaclean-safety-recall.

recalls.metaDescription: Deale International recalls CasaClean Handheld Steamers due to burn risks. Stop use immediately and contact for refund instructions.

recalls.Description: BISSELL is recalling several models of its Steam Shot Handheld Steam Cleaners, specifically from the 39N7 and 2994 series. These products feature 'STEAM SHOT' or 'POWER STEAMER' printed on the side, with model numbers located on the bottom rating label. Available colors include green, pink, blue, orange, white, black, purple, and red.
recalls.Title: BISSELL Recalls Over 3 Million Steam Shot Handheld Steam Cleaners Due to Burn Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: Consumers should stop using the affected steam cleaners immediately. For assistance, contact BISSELL toll-free at 855-417-7001 from 9 a.m. to 9 p.m. ET Monday through Friday, and 9 a.m. to 8 p.m. ET on Saturday. Alternatively, email RecallNA@bissell.com or visit www.BISSELL.com/steamshotrecall for more details.

Products.Name: BISSELL Steam Shot Handheld Steam Cleaners
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 3.2 million (In addition, about 355,000 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled BISSELL Steam Shot Handheld Steam Cleaner (color may vary)
Model number is printed on label located on the bottom of the product

Injuries.Name: BISSELL has received 183 reports of hot water or steam escaping from the product, including 157 reports of minor burn injuries.

Retailers.Name: Available at Target, Walmart, and various department and home goods stores across the country, as well as online at www.bissell.com, www.amazon.com, www.hsn.com, and other retailers from August 2008 through May 2024, priced between $35 and $40, or $70 for a two-pack.

Importers.Name: BISSELL Homecare Inc., of Grand Rapids, Michigan

Hazards.Name: Burn hazard due to expulsion of hot water or steam

Remedies.Name: Consumers can receive a $60 credit for future purchases at BISSELL.com or a $40 refund for each recalled unit. Visit www.BISSELL.com/steamshotrecall for registration and instructions on how to cut the cord and submit a photo of the steam cleaner.

recalls.metaDescription: BISSELL recalls over 3 million Steam Shot Handheld Steam Cleaners due to burn hazards. Learn how to get a refund or credit.

recalls.Description: Empower Brands has expanded the recall of all BLACK+DECKER Model HGS011 Easy Garment Steamers, including those repaired in the previous recall from November 2022. These steamers, available in various colors, are approximately 11 inches tall and 6 inches wide, featuring a prominent handle. Model numbers can be found on the bottom of the steamer and packaging, while UPC codes are located on the sides, back, and bottom of the packaging.
recalls.Title: Major Recall of Two Million BLACK+DECKER Garment Steamers Due to Burn Risk; Refund Available
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers should reach out to Empower Brands at 800-990-5298, Monday through Friday from 8 a.m. to 4:30 p.m. CT, email hgsrecall@brandprotectplus.com, or visit www.prodprotect.com/recall or www.blackanddeckerappliances.com and select "Safety Notices" for further details.

Products.Name: BLACK+DECKER Model HGS011 Easy Garment Steamer
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 1.6 million (518,500 units were previously recalled in November 2022)

Images.Caption: Recalled BLACK+DECKER HGS011 Easy Garment Steamer
Recalled BLACK+DECKER HGS011F Easy Garment Steamer
Recalled BLACK+DECKER HGS011S Easy Garment Steamer

Injuries.Name: Since the recall was announced in November 2022, Empower Brands has received 317 reports of hot water expelling from the recalled steamers, including 82 reports of burn injuries, seven of which were second-degree burns. 94 of these incident reports involved units that were repaired as part of the original recall or models featuring the updated design, including 19 burn injuries.

Retailers.Name: Available at Walmart, Target, Amazon, Bed Bath & Beyond, and other retailers across the nation, including online at www.walmart.com, www.target.com, www.amazon.com, www.bedbathandbeyond.com, and www.blackanddeckerappliances.com from June 2021 to February 2024, priced between $14 and $23.

Importers.Name: Spectrum Brands Inc., of Middleton, Wis., owns subsidiary Empower Brands LLC

Hazards.Name: Risk of burn due to hot water expulsion or leakage during use.

Remedies.Name: Consumers must stop using the recalled steamers and contact Empower Brands for a full refund. Reports of burn injuries have continued, even for units repaired in the previous recall. The remedy has now changed to a refund for all units, including those that received a replacement part.

recalls.metaDescription: Recall alert: Two million BLACK+DECKER garment steamers pose burn risks. Refunds available. Act now for safety.

recalls.Description:
This recall affects several handheld garment steamers from different brands, including Steamfast models SF-425, SF-435, SF-440, SF-445, and SF-447; Vornado model VS-410; and Sharper Image model SI-428. The steamers come in various colors, such as white, black, light pink, hot pink, sage green, orange, and teal blue. These appliances are designed to emit hot steam to remove wrinkles from fabrics, and the brand name and model number are clearly labeled on each unit.

The recalled steamers can release hot water from the nozzle during operation, creating a significant burn hazard for users. Consumers are urged to take immediate action regarding these products to ensure their safety.
recalls.Title: Vornado Recalls Two Million Handheld Garment Steamers Due to Burn Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact Vornado at 888-240-2768 from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET Monday through Friday, or visit www.vornado.com/recalls/hhgs. Additional information can be found at www.vornado.com, www.steamfast.com, or www.sihomecomfort.com under the "Product Recalls" section.

Products.Name: Steamfast Handheld Garment Steamer SF-425
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 2 million (In addition, about 13,000 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled Steamfast Steamer, Model SF-425
Recalled Steamfast Steamer, Model SF-435 (multiple colors)
Recalled Steamfast Steamer, Model SF-440
Recalled Steamfast Steamer, Model SF-445
Recalled Steamfast Steamer, Model SF-447

Injuries.Name: Vornado has received 122 reports of hot water spraying or spitting from the steam nozzle, including 23 reports of burn injuries.

Importers.Name: Vornado Air LLC, of Andover, Kansas

Hazards.Name: Risk of burns from hot water expulsion

Remedies.Name: Full refund or free replacement handheld garment steamer

recalls.metaDescription: Vornado recalls two million handheld garment steamers due to serious burn risks. Stop using and contact for refunds or replacements.

recalls.Description: Vornado has expanded its recall to include an additional 1.75 million Steamfast brand Home and Away Travel Steam Irons, specifically models SF-717, SF-720, and SF-727. The model numbers can be found printed on the back of the irons, with 'Steamfast' labeled on the white units. Consumers are urged to take immediate action regarding these products.
recalls.Title: Vornado Expands Recall of 1.75 Million Steamfast Travel Steam Irons Due to Fire, Burn, and Shock Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can contact Vornado at 866-827-3362, available from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday. Additionally, visit www.steamfast.com/recalls/travelirons to initiate a recall submission. More information can also be found at www.vornado.com or www.steamfast.com by clicking on 'Product Recalls' at the top of the page.

Products.Name: Steamfast Home & Away Travel Steam Irons
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 1.75 million (In addition, about 9,000 in Canada) (About 275,000 Steamfast Home & Away Travel Steam Irons manufactured before March 2017 were previously recalled in April 2023)

Images.Caption: Recalled Steamfast Iron Model Side View
Recalled Steamfast Iron Models SF-717, SF-720, and SF-727
Model number on back of recalled Steamfast irons

Injuries.Name: Vornado has received a total of 74 reports of incidents, including 18 additional reports of the iron's power cord smoking, sparking, catching fire, or overheating at the power cord, or of the power cord becoming damaged. Vornado has received two reports of minor shocks.

Retailers.Name: Available at Walmart, Bed Bath & Beyond, and various other retailers nationwide, as well as online at Amazon.com, Vornado.com, and Steamfast.com from January 2009 through September 2023, priced between $13 and $30.

Importers.Name: Vornado Air LLC, of Andover, Kansas

Hazards.Name: Potential fire and burn hazards due to damaged power cords and shock hazards from exposed copper wires.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should cease use of the recalled irons and register for the recall online to receive either a refund or a free replacement.

recalls.metaDescription: Vornado recalls 1.75 million Steamfast Travel Steam Irons due to fire and shock hazards. Act now for a refund or replacement.
